mission.title: karmada: Discussion about adding propagate priority during scheduling
mission.description: Discussion about adding propagate priority during scheduling. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current karmada deployment
steps.description:
Verify your karmada version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n karmada -l app.kubernetes.io/name=karmada
helm list -n karmada 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working karmada installation.

steps.title: Review karmada configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant karmada configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n karmada -l app.kubernetes.io/name=karmada
kubectl get configmap -n karmada -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=karmada
```
**What would you like to be added**:

Add a new field `PropagatePriority` into `ClusterAffinity` so that user could have a propagating preference with multiple levels of priority. 

**User Story**:

1.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Discussion about adding propagate priority during scheduling
steps.description:
I think it's not just for dividing replicas. Let me explain this field.

When propagating, we have some `constraints`, e.g., `spreadConstraints`, `availableReplicas`, etc. What we are going to do is try to find a solution under the `constraints`. 

`PropagatePriority` aims to tell us in which set we could look for solutions. Imagine we get a priority list with set A, set B and set C by descending order. 

1. We try to find solutions in set A under the `constraints`.
2. If failed, maybe due to

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n karmada -l app.kubernetes.io/name=karmada
kubectl get events -n karmada --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Discussion about adding propagate priority during scheduling" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
I think it's not just for dividing replicas. Let me explain this field.

When propagating, we have some `constraints`, e.g., `spreadConstraints`, `availableReplicas`, etc. What we are going to do is try to find a solution under the `constraints`. 

`PropagatePriority` aims to tell us in which set we could look for solutions.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with karmada installed or the issue environment reproducible.
